name: A American Debt Consolidation
description_short: ACCC is a non-profit credit counseling organization offering free debt management programs, bankruptcy counseling, and financial guidance to help consumers reduce debt and regain financial stability.
description_long:
American Consumer Credit Counseling (ACCC) is a non-profit organization providing free and low-cost financial counseling services to individuals and families struggling with debt. The organization operates with a mission to provide compassionate, professional guidance without pressuring clients into unwanted services. ACCC explicitly states it does not loan money, distinguishing itself as a counseling and education resource rather than a lending entity.

ACCC offers multiple debt relief and financial counseling services including debt management programs (DMPs), bankruptcy pre- and post-filing counseling, reverse mortgage counseling, first-time homebuyer courses, and student loan guidance. Their primary service is their debt management program, which involves credit counseling designed to help clients significantly reduce interest rates and monthly payments on existing debt. The organization provides free consultations with counselors to explore options tailored to individual financial situations.

ACCC distinguishes itself through its Better Business Bureau A+ rating, non-profit status, and emphasis on client autonomy and transparency. Customer reviews consistently highlight specific counselor names and praise the professionalism, lack of pressure, knowledge, and sympathetic approach of staff. The organization operates a client login portal for enrolled members to access program information, due dates, benefits, and documents. Their phone-based consultation model (800-769-3571) makes services accessible without requiring in-person visits.

As a non-profit credit counseling organization, ACCC is appropriate for consumers seeking legitimate debt management support without predatory lending or settlement schemes. However, potential clients should understand that debt management programs require active participation, creditor cooperation, and typically take 3-5 years to complete. The free consultation model may limit initial depth of analysis compared to more comprehensive financial planning services, and outcomes depend significantly on individual compliance and creditor willingness to negotiate.
pros: Non-profit organization with Better Business Bureau A+ rating and no financial incentive to oversell services
Free initial consultation with no obligation to enroll in paid programs
Debt management program designed to reduce interest rates and monthly payments without taking new loans
Multiple counseling services including bankruptcy, homebuyer education, student loan, and reverse mortgage counseling
Established client login portal providing ongoing access to program documents, due dates, and benefits
Emphasis on client autonomy—reviews explicitly note lack of pressure to sign contracts
Licensed, knowledgeable counselors with consistently positive individual reviews mentioning specific staff by name
cons: Debt management programs typically require 3-5 years to complete, not a fast solution
Success depends on creditor willingness to negotiate—not guaranteed for all accounts
Limited information on website about specific interest rate reductions or payment reduction ranges
Phone-based model may limit initial assessment depth compared to comprehensive in-person financial planning
Form requests broad debt range categories ($1K-$5K, $51K-$100K+) rather than precise amount needed for tailored quotes
best_for: Consumers with $5K-$100K in credit card debt seeking legitimate debt consolidation without predatory terms
Individuals considering bankruptcy who need pre-filing counseling and education
First-time homebuyers seeking HUD-approved counseling and education
Anyone seeking free financial guidance before committing to paid debt relief programs
diagnosis: ACCC is best for consumers with moderate-to-high credit card debt ($5K+) who want legitimate, non-predatory debt consolidation through a non-profit with professional counseling and proven track record. The main caveat is that debt management programs require multi-year commitment and creditor cooperation, making them unsuitable for those seeking rapid debt elimination or who have accounts already in severe default.
services: Debt management program (DMP) with interest rate and payment reduction negotiation
Free debt counseling consultation and financial assessment
Post-bankruptcy counseling and financial recovery guidance
Client portal access for enrolled DMP members
Ongoing support and guidance throughout debt repayment process
Credit counseling focused on understanding credit and financial management
meta_description: ACCC offers free debt management, bankruptcy counseling, and financial guidance to help consumers reduce debt and regain financial stability.
